title: Ensure that the --client-ca-file argument is set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --client-ca-file argument is set as appropriate
input_row.description: Setup TLS connection on the API server.
input_row.rationale: API server communication contains sensitive parameters that should remain encrypted in transit. Configure the API server to serve only HTTPS traffic. If --client-ca-file argument is set, any request presenting a client certificate signed by one of the authorities in the client-ca-file is authenticated with an identity corresponding to the CommonName of the client certificate.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --client-ca-file argument exists and it is set as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and set up the TLS connection on the apiserver. Then, edit the /etc/kubernetes/apiserver file on the master node and set the KUBE_API_ARGS parameter to include "--client-ca-file=<path/to/client-ca-file>": KUBE_API_ARGS="--client-ca-file=<path/to/client-ca-file>" Based on your system, restart the kube-apiserver service. For example: systemctl restart kube-apiserver.service Impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for your Kubernetes cluster deployment. Default Value: By default, --client-ca-file argument is not set.  71 | P a g e References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. http://rootsquash.com/2016/05/10/securing-the-kubernetes-api/ 3. https://github.com/kelseyhightower/docker-kubernetes-tls-guide
input_row.impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for your Kubernetes cluster deployment. Default Value: By default, --client-ca-file argument is not set.  71 | P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_client_ca_file_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to verify that the `--client-ca-file` argument is set.
- In modern Kubernetes deployments, the API server is typically run as a static Pod. Use the following command to retrieve the current configuration:
  - `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o json`
- Inspect the `spec.containers[].command` or `spec.containers[].args` fields of the kube-apiserver Pod manifest to confirm the presence of the `--client-ca-file` flag and that it points to a valid file path.
- Alternatively, if the API server is managed outside of Kubernetes (e.g., as a systemd service), retrieve the running process arguments using node-level tooling, but this is less common in modern clusters.
- Pass Criteria: The kube-apiserver must have the `--client-ca-file` flag set to a non-empty, valid file path.
- Fail Criteria: The `--client-ca-file` flag is missing, empty, or commented out.
- Example automated check (for static Pod):
  1. Identify the kube-apiserver Pod:  
     `kubectl -n kube-system get pods | grep kube-apiserver`
  2. Get the Pod spec:  
     `kubectl -n kube-system get pod <kube-apiserver-pod-name> -o json | jq '.spec.containers[].command'`
  3. Confirm `--client-ca-file=<path>` is present in the command/args list.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
